items.title: Parents slam 'weak' school response after 21 girls targeted in deepfake scandal

children.content: Two parents of girls at a private school have criticised the institution's response to a deepfake incident, saying they were encouraged not to tell their daughters they had been identified in material shared in a boys' group chat.

items.title: AI deepfakes are in our schools. What's the right way to handle them?

children.content: Deepfake content, made from artificial intelligence, is increasingly circulating in schools. But what can parents do if their children are the target?

items.title: Greens sound alarm after salmon farm uses tonnes of antibiotics in waterway

children.content: Almost 3 tonnes of the antibiotic florfenicol were used at one Tasmanian salmon farm lease in just two months, with information on its use across eight other sites yet to be released to the public by authorities.

items.title: Memorial held on 30th anniversary of Port Arthur massacre

children.content: Thirty years on from the Port Arthur tragedy, survivor Jane Scholefield had a simple message for those still bearing the scars of that day — keep love at the centre of mourning.

articles.title: They were the first victims of the Port Arthur massacre. Their family has never forgotten

children.content: Seascape guesthouse owners Sally and David Martin were the first people murdered at Port Arthur in 1996. On the 30th anniversary of the tragedy, their granddaughter tells of how the family has refused to be defined by it.

image.altText: A man and woman pose for a photo in front of a waterfall.

articles.title: Walter Mikac lost his wife and daughters at Port Arthur. Thirty years on, this is his message

children.content: On April 28, 1996, a gunman killed 35 people at Port Arthur. Walter Mikac's wife and two daughters were among them. Thirty years on, he says gun control is as important as ever.

image.altText: a bald man in a light blue t shirt

gallery.altText: Colourful flowers left on a concrete ledge.

items.title: Port Arthur gun reform was swift. Bondi is proving a different challenge

children.content: Firearms reforms were swift and lasting after Port Arthur. But Bondi has proved a far more challenging political process for the federal government. So where are we at with the gun law reforms?

items.title: Is a second Tamar River crossing ever going to happen?

items.title: King's speech to Congress hits on several Trump controversies

items.title: NT Police say some in community 'absolutely know' where suspected abductor is

children.content: Northern Territory Police say some people in the community "absolutely know" where missing five-year-old girl Sharon Granites's suspected abductor is, and are imploring anyone with information that could help find them to come forward.

articles.title: As search for missing 5yo Sharon continues, a clearer picture of that night emerges

children.content: Five-year-old Sharon Granites vanished from an Alice Springs town camp late on Saturday night, with police alleging she has been abducted. Here's what we know about the night she went missing.

articles.title: Criminal history of man police are searching for over missing 5yo girl revealed

items.title: 'Double-headed monster' set to push up prices at the check-out

children.content: Companies that use plastic packaging are facing price pressures and supply disruptions, which they warn will likely mean higher costs for consumers. The cost of food-grade resin out of Singapore, which is used in milk bottles, has increased 110 per cent since the start of the Middle East war.
